result:
If I could change just one thing, I’d want every person—regardless of where or when they’re born—to have reliable, compassionate access to high-quality education and the mental-health support needed to make use of it.  

That single push would set off an almost uncountable cascade of improvements: better health outcomes, stronger economies, less violent conflict, faster scientific progress, more resilient communities. It wouldn’t solve every problem overnight, but it bends the arc of possibility in a direction where every other good idea has a much better chance of being invented, debated, tried, and scaled with wisdom.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
